scope: Elixir/OTP actor runtime core: Application supervision tree, PeerServer (per-actor GenServer), PeerSupervisor (DynamicSupervisor), PeerRegistry + SessionRegistry, WorkerSupervisor, DeadLetter queue, esr:// URI parser.

test_summary.command: mix test test/esr/application_restore_adapters_test.exs test/esr/application_restore_test.exs test/esr/application_test.exs test/esr/dead_letter_test.exs test/esr/peer_registry_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_action_dispatch_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_emit_ack_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_esr_channel_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_event_handling_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_invoke_command_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_pause_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_persist_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_retry_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_test.exs test/esr/peer_server_tool_invoke_test.exs test/esr/peer_supervisor_test.exs test/esr/session_registry_test.exs test/esr/uri_test.exs test/esr/worker_supervisor_test.exs test/esr/workspaces_registry_test.exs
warnings: lib/esr/topology/instantiator.ex:332 — non-grouped defp issue_init_directive/2 clauses (stylistic, does not affect behavior)

supervision_tree.restore_on_start: After start_link returns, Esr.Application loads $ESRD_HOME/default/workspaces.yaml into Esr.Resource.Workspace.Registry and $ESRD_HOME/default/adapters.yaml via WorkerSupervisor.ensure_adapter; feishu adapters trigger Task-spawned feishu-app-session topology auto-restore (2s grace).

children_in_order.name: Registry (Esr.Entity.Registry)
children_in_order.note: must be up before any PeerServer registers

children_in_order.name: EsrWeb.Endpoint
children_in_order.note: started last so channels serve only after registries are up

state_struct_fields: state :: map (rehydrated from Esr.Persistence.Ets table :esr_actor_states on init)
pending_events :: [envelope] (newest-at-head; replayed in arrival order on resume; capped @pause_queue_limit=1000)
handle_call: :describe -> snapshot map {actor_id, actor_type, handler_module, paused, state}
:resume -> replays pending_events in FIFO order + [:esr,:actor,:resumed] telemetry
handle_info: {:inbound_event, envelope} -> dedup-by-idempotency_key (payload.args.idempotency_key); HandlerRouter.call (one retry on :handler_timeout); persist via Persistence.Ets BEFORE dispatching returned actions; on retry_exhausted -> DeadLetter.enqueue
{:tool_invoke, req_id, tool, args, reply_pid} -> build_emit_for_tool (reply/react/send_file/_echo) -> emit_and_track, store {req_id, reply_pid} in pending_tool_reqs
{:directive_ack, %{id,payload}} when id in pending_tool_reqs -> push tool_result to reply_pid; unsubscribe
{:directive_deadline, id} when id in pending_tool_reqs -> tool_result timeout error
{:EXIT, _, _} -> swallowed (stray EXITs; PeerServer doesn't link workers)
action_dispatch: %{type:'emit', adapter} -> broadcast envelope to adapter:<name>/<actor_id> via EsrWeb.Endpoint; subscribe to directive_ack:<id>; Process.send_after(:directive_deadline)
%{type:'route', target, msg} -> Registry.lookup + send({:inbound_event, %{payload: msg}}); [:esr,:route,:target_missing] on miss
peer_server.persistence: invoke_handler writes the returned new_state into Esr.Persistence.Ets @table :esr_actor_states via PersistStore.put BEFORE dispatching actions (spec §7.4 persist-then-emit). init/1 rehydrates from the same table — prior state wins over initial_state for fresh spawns.
peer_server.retry_policy: call_with_retry does one retry on :handler_timeout; others are non-retryable. Exhaustion -> DeadLetter.enqueue reason: :handler_retry_exhausted
peer_server.tool_invoke_v02: Built-in tool builders: reply (chat_id+text -> feishu send_message), react (message_id+emoji_type -> feishu react), send_file (chat_id+file_path -> feishu send_file), _echo (requires state.chat_id, synthesises reply). Unknown tool -> error.

Esr.Entity.Registry.maps: actor_id :: String -> pid (one PeerServer per key). Wrapper API: register/2 (must be self-registration), lookup/1, list_all/0. Registry auto-cleans on monitor DOWN.

Esr.SessionRegistry.type: Named GenServer + public named ETS set (:esr_session_registry, read_concurrency: true)
Esr.SessionRegistry.maps: session_id :: String -> %{ws_pid, chat_ids, app_ids, workspace, status: :online|:offline, last_seen_ms}
Esr.SessionRegistry.notify_session/2: looks up session_id; if online, send(ws_pid, {:push_envelope, envelope}); else {:error, :offline|:not_registered}
Esr.SessionRegistry.design_note_S1: peer_pid is deliberately NOT stored — authoritative lookup is Registry.lookup(Esr.Entity.Registry, "thread:<sid>"). Avoids drift between two stores.

dead_letter.type: GenServer backed by :queue with size counter
dead_letter.eviction: oldest-first (FIFO drop) on overflow, size stays at max
captures_messages_when: handler_retry_exhausted — PeerServer calls DeadLetter.enqueue after the second HandlerRouter.call attempt fails (source=actor_id, metadata={handler_module, last_error})

(documented targets per moduledoc) unknown Route/Emit target and adapter directive failure after retries — currently only the handler_retry_exhausted caller site is wired in lib/; other two captured via external subsystems not in this module scope
dead_letter.telemetry: [:esr, :deadletter, :event] on every enqueue with measurements {size} and metadata {reason, target, id}

peer_supervisor.child_restart: :transient (normal exit not respawned; abnormal restarts within supervisor intensity)
api: stop_peer/1 (looks up via PeerRegistry, terminates child; {:error, :not_found} on miss)

worker_supervisor.purpose: On-demand launcher for Python adapter_runner and handler_worker subprocesses (Phase 8f; lets final_gate.sh --live bootstrap without external fixtures).
worker_supervisor.idempotency: Checks tracked_alive? first (pid_alive? via `kill -0`), then external pidfile (/tmp/esr-worker-<slug>.pid) written by scripts/spawn_scenario_workers.sh. Only when both absent does it spawn via scripts/spawn_worker.sh (daemonises + prints pid).
worker_supervisor.shutdown: terminate/2 SIGTERMs then SIGKILLs every tracked pid (500ms gap). Pidfile dir is NOT cleaned.

restore_semantics.trigger: Application.start/2 after Supervisor.start_link, only if Application.get_env(:esr, :restore_on_start, true)
restore_semantics.workspaces: load_workspaces_from_disk/1 reads <home>/default/workspaces.yaml via Esr.Resource.Workspace.Registry.load_from_file -> for each {name, ws} put into registry. Missing file returns :ok.
restore_semantics.adapters: restore_adapters_from_disk/2 reads <home>/default/adapters.yaml via YamlElixir; for each instance calls opts[:spawn_fn] (default = WorkerSupervisor.ensure_adapter with default_adapter_ws_url()). If type==feishu and config.app_id is non-empty, spawns Task.start/0 that sleeps 2s, then Topology.Registry.get_artifact("feishu-app-session") + Topology.Instantiator.instantiate with %{"app_id"=>app_id}. Logs info/warning on outcome.
restore_semantics.actor_state: PeerServer.init/1 independently rehydrates per-actor state from :esr_actor_states ETS table via Esr.Persistence.Ets.get — prior state wins, initial_state is the fresh-spawn fallback.

key_invariants: Supervision root Esr.Supervisor uses :one_for_one — one subsystem crash does not cascade to siblings (Track D session-isolation).
PeerRegistry must be alive before any PeerServer registers via {:via, Registry, ...}; order in children list enforces this.
PeerServer registration is :unique — duplicate actor_id causes {:error, {:already_registered, _}}.
PeerServer persists new_state to Esr.Persistence.Ets BEFORE dispatching any returned actions (spec §7.4 persist-then-emit).
Handler retry is exactly once on :handler_timeout; every other error is non-retryable.
Dedup keys bounded at 1000 entries with FIFO eviction (state.dedup_keys MapSet + state.dedup_order :queue).
Paused PeerServer queues inbound_events up to @pause_queue_limit=1000; further events are silently dropped; resume replays in arrival order then processes new input.
Emit directive flow: subscribe to directive_ack:<id> BEFORE broadcasting so a fast ack lands in mailbox; Process.send_after fires :directive_deadline after directive_timeout.
Emit topic lookup prefers an actor already bound under adapter:<name>/* in Esr.AdapterHub.Registry; falls back to adapter:<name>/<self.actor_id>.
Emit adapter='esr-channel' is synthetic — short-circuits through SessionRegistry.notify_session (v0.2 §3.3), does not hit AdapterHub.
SessionRegistry intentionally does NOT store peer_pid (reviewer S1) — authoritative lookup is Registry.lookup(Esr.Entity.Registry, "thread:<sid>").

PeerRegistry.register/2 refuses to register a foreign pid ({:error, :cannot_register_other_pid}) — Elixir Registry only registers the calling process; PeerServer.init/1 calls register(actor_id, self()).
DeadLetter eviction keeps size pinned at max_entries when full (drops oldest, inserts newest) — size never exceeds max.
WorkerSupervisor.ensure_* is idempotent across in-process tracking and external pidfiles.
Esr.Uri requires non-empty host; type must be one of {actor, adapter, handler, command, interface}.

PeerServer sets trap_exit=true so terminate/2 fires for [:esr,:peer_server,:stopped] telemetry and feishu_thread_proxy session_killed log; stray {:EXIT, _, _} info messages are swallowed to avoid FunctionClauseError (reviewer S8).

Restore is best-effort: missing workspaces.yaml/adapters.yaml is :ok, per-adapter spawn errors are logged-and-continued, and feishu-app-session auto-restore is deferred via Task with 2s grace so Application.start returns promptly.
